001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
023
024import java.lang.reflect.*;
025import java.util.*;
026import java.util.function.*;
027import java.util.logging.*;
028
029import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
030import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
031import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.util.*;
032
033import jakarta.servlet.*;
034
035/**
036 * Implements the child resources of a {@link Rest}-annotated class.
037 *
038 * <p>
039 * Holds a registry of {@link RestContext} instances keyed by {@link RestContext#getPath() composed path}.
040 * Reads (request routing via {@link #findMatch(RestSession.Builder)} and the {@link #asMap()} accessor) are
041 * lock-free against a volatile copy-on-write snapshot; mutations
042 * ({@link #addChild(Class) addChild} / {@link #removeChild(String) removeChild}) synchronize on an internal
043 * write lock and atomically replace the snapshot. This makes runtime child management safe even while
044 * requests are in flight on other threads.
045 *
046 * <p>
047 * Supports lazy-init children registered via {@link Builder#addLazy(Class, String)} — these children have their
048 * {@link RestContext} constructed on the first matching request rather than at parent startup.  The routing entry
049 * (path prefix matcher) is always populated at startup; only the full context build is deferred.
050 *
051 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
052 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestAnnotatedClassBasics">@Rest-Annotated Class Basics</a>
053 * </ul>
054 */
055public class RestChildren {
056
057   private static final Logger LOGGER = Logger.getLogger(RestChildren.class.getName());
058
059   /**
060    * Holder for a lazy-init child resource entry.
061    *
062    * <p>
063    * At parent startup, only the path prefix and {@link UrlPathMatcher} are populated.  The full
064    * {@link RestContext} is built on the first request that matches the path prefix; subsequent requests
065    * reuse the cached context.  Two concurrent first-requests serialize on {@link #lock} so that exactly
066    * one materialization runs.
067    *
068    * <p>
069    * If materialization throws, the exception propagates to the caller as a {@link jakarta.servlet.ServletException};
070    * the {@link #materialized} field is left {@code null}, so the next request will retry.
071    */
072   static class LazyChildEntry {
073
074      final Class<?> resourceClass;
075      final String path;
076      final UrlPathMatcher pathMatcher;
077      final RestContext parent;
078      final BeanStore beanStore;
079      final ServletConfig servletConfig;
080
081      /** Written once under {@link #lock}; volatile for lock-free reads. */
082      @SuppressWarnings({
083         "java:S3077" // Publish-once reference: assigned once under double-checked locking in materialize(); the RestContext is fully built before assignment, so volatile safe-publication is sufficient.
084      })
085      volatile RestContext materialized;
086
087      private final Object lock = new Object();
088
089      LazyChildEntry(Class<?> resourceClass, String path, RestContext parent, BeanStore beanStore, ServletConfig servletConfig) {
090         this.resourceClass = resourceClass;
091         this.path = path;
092         var p = path;
093         if (! p.endsWith("/*"))
094            p += "/*";
095         this.pathMatcher = UrlPathMatcher.of(p);
096         this.parent = parent;
097         this.beanStore = beanStore;
098         this.servletConfig = servletConfig;
099      }
100
101      boolean isMaterialized() { return materialized != null; }
102
103      /**
104       * Returns the materialized {@link RestContext}, building it if this is the first call.
105       *
106       * <p>
107       * Thread-safe: at most one thread runs the construction; others block on {@link #lock}.
108       * If construction fails the field is left {@code null}; the next request will retry.
109       *
110       * @return The materialized context. Never {@code null} on success.
111       * @throws ServletException If context construction fails.
112       */
113      RestContext materialize() throws ServletException {
114         RestContext rc = materialized;
115         if (rc != null)
116            return rc;
117         synchronized (lock) {
118            rc = materialized;
119            if (rc != null)
120               return rc;
121            LOGGER.info(() -> "Lazy REST child materializing: " + resourceClass.getName() + " at path '" + path + "'");
122            try {
123               rc = buildChildContext(parent, beanStore, servletConfig, resourceClass, null, "");
124               rc.postInit();
125               rc.postInitChildFirst();
126            } catch (Exception e) {
127               throw new ServletException("Failed to lazily materialize child REST context for " + resourceClass.getName(), unwrapThrowable(e));
128            }
129            materialized = rc;
130            LOGGER.info(() -> "Lazy REST child materialized: " + resourceClass.getName() + " at path '" + path + "'");
131            return rc;
132         }
133      }
134
135      /**
136       * Resolves the path prefix for a resource class by walking its annotation hierarchy.
137       *
138       * <p>
139       * Replicates the most-derived-first walk that {@link RestContext} performs during full construction,
140       * but does so without instantiating a {@link RestContext} — enabling startup-time path extraction
141       * for lazy children.
142       *
143       * @param resourceClass The resource class. Must not be {@code null}.
144       * @return The trimmed path (without leading slash). Empty string if no {@code @Rest(path=...)} is found.
145       */
146      static String resolvePathForClass(Class<?> resourceClass) {
147         for (Class<?> c = resourceClass; c != null; c = c.getSuperclass()) {
148            Rest r = c.getDeclaredAnnotation(Rest.class);
149            if (r != null && isNotEmpty(r.path()))
150               return trimLeadingSlashes(r.path());
151         }
152         for (Class<?> iface : resourceClass.getInterfaces()) {
153            Rest r = iface.getDeclaredAnnotation(Rest.class);
154            if (r != null && isNotEmpty(r.path()))
155               return trimLeadingSlashes(r.path());
156         }
157         return "";
158      }
159   }
160
161   /**
162    * Builder class.
163    */
164   public static class Builder {
165
166      final RestContext parent;
167      final BeanStore beanStore;
168      final ServletConfig servletConfig;
169      final List<RestContext> list;
170      final List<LazyChildEntry> lazyList;
171
172      /**
173       * Constructor.
174       *
175       * @param parent The parent {@link RestContext} that owns these children. Used for parent-context wiring
176       *    on dynamically added children. May be {@code null} when constructing from contexts already built
177       *    externally and runtime add/remove is not required.
178       * @param beanStore The bean store used for dependency injection on dynamically added children. Must not be
179       *    {@code null}.
180       * @param servletConfig The {@link ServletConfig} passed through to dynamically added child contexts.
181       *    May be {@code null}.
182       */
183      protected Builder(RestContext parent, BeanStore beanStore, ServletConfig servletConfig) {
184         this.parent = parent;
185         this.beanStore = beanStore;
186         this.servletConfig = servletConfig;
187         this.list = list();
188         this.lazyList = list();
189      }
190
191      /**
192       * Returns the bean store used by this builder.
193       *
194       * @return The bean store used by this builder.
195       */
196      public BeanStore beanStore() {
197         return beanStore;
198      }
199
200      /**
201       * Adds an eager child resource to this builder.
202       *
203       * @param value The REST context of the child resource.
204       * @return This object.
205       */
206      public Builder add(RestContext value) {
207         list.add(value);
208         return this;
209      }
210
211      /**
212       * Registers a lazy child resource entry.
213       *
214       * <p>
215       * The path is pre-resolved from the resource class so that routing works immediately at parent startup.
216       * The full {@link RestContext} is built on the first matching request.
217       *
218       * @param resourceClass The resource class to materialize lazily. Must not be {@code null}.
219       * @param path The pre-resolved path prefix (without leading slash). Use {@code ""} to read from
220       *    {@link Rest#path() @Rest(path)} on the resource class.
221       * @return This object.
222       */
223      public Builder addLazy(Class<?> resourceClass, String path) {
224         var resolvedPath = isNotEmpty(path) ? trimLeadingSlashes(path) : LazyChildEntry.resolvePathForClass(resourceClass);
225         lazyList.add(new LazyChildEntry(resourceClass, resolvedPath, parent, beanStore, servletConfig));
226         return this;
227      }
228
229      /**
230       * Builds the children.
231       *
232       * @return A new {@link RestChildren}.
233       */
234      public RestChildren build() {
235         return new RestChildren(this);
236      }
237   }
238
239   /**
240    * Static creator.
241    *
242    * @param parent The parent {@link RestContext}. Required for runtime {@link #addChild(Class) addChild} support
243    *    (may be {@code null} otherwise).
244    * @param beanStore The bean store used for child instantiation.
245    * @param servletConfig The {@link ServletConfig} propagated to dynamically added children. May be {@code null}.
246    * @return A new builder for this object.
247    */
248   public static Builder create(RestContext parent, BeanStore beanStore, ServletConfig servletConfig) {
249      return new Builder(parent, beanStore, servletConfig);
250   }
251
252   private final RestContext parent;
253   private final BeanStore beanStore;
254   private final ServletConfig servletConfig;
255   private final Object writeLock = new Object();
256
257   /** Eager-init children keyed by composed path. Copy-on-write; volatile for lock-free reads. */
258   @SuppressWarnings({
259      "java:S3077" // Copy-on-write snapshot: the map reference is replaced wholesale under writeLock and never compound-mutated, so volatile safe-publication is sufficient for lock-free reads.
260   })
261   private volatile Map<String,RestContext> children;
262
263   /**
264    * Lazy-init children keyed by path prefix.
265    *
266    * <p>
267    * These entries are registered at parent startup for routing purposes; their {@link RestContext} bodies
268    * are built on first use.  Once materialized, the entry's {@link LazyChildEntry#materialized} field is set
269    * but the entry remains in this map so subsequent reads are lock-free (volatile field read on the entry).
270    * This map itself is never mutated after construction, so no copy-on-write is needed.
271    */
272   private final Map<String,LazyChildEntry> lazyEntries;
273
274   /**
275    * Constructor.
276    *
277    * @param builder The builder containing the settings for this object.
278    */
279   public RestChildren(Builder builder) {
280      this.parent = builder.parent;
281      this.beanStore = builder.beanStore;
282      this.servletConfig = builder.servletConfig;
283      var initial = new LinkedHashMap<String,RestContext>();
284      for (var rc : builder.list)
285         initial.put(rc.getPath(), rc);
286      this.children = Collections.unmodifiableMap(initial);
287      var lazyMap = new LinkedHashMap<String,LazyChildEntry>();
288      for (var e : builder.lazyList)
289         lazyMap.put(e.path, e);
290      this.lazyEntries = Collections.unmodifiableMap(lazyMap);
291   }
292
293   /**
294    * Returns the children in this object as a map.
295    *
296    * <p>
297    * The keys are the {@link RestContext#getPath() paths} of the child contexts. The returned map is an
298    * unmodifiable snapshot taken at the time of the call; subsequent {@link #addChild(Class) addChild} /
299    * {@link #removeChild(String) removeChild} calls do not affect this view.
300    *
301    * @return The children as an unmodifiable map.
302    */
303   public Map<String,RestContext> asMap() {
304      return children;
305   }
306
307   /**
308    * Called during servlet destruction on all children to invoke all {@link RestDestroy} and {@link Servlet#destroy()} methods.
309    *
310    * <p>
311    * Lazy children that were never materialized are silently skipped (no context was ever built for them).
312    */
313   public void destroy() {
314      for (var r : children.values())
315         destroyChild(r);
316      for (var e : lazyEntries.values()) {
317         var rc = e.materialized;  // volatile read — safe without lock
318         if (rc != null)
319            destroyChild(rc);
320      }
321   }
322
323   /**
324    * Looks through the registered children of this object and returns the best match.
325    *
326    * <p>
327    * Eager children are checked first (lock-free volatile read), then lazy entries.  A matching lazy entry
328    * triggers {@link LazyChildEntry#materialize()} which builds the full {@link RestContext} on first call
329    * (blocking concurrent first-requests until construction completes) and caches the result.
330    *
331    * @param builder The HTTP call builder.
332    * @return The child that best matches the call, or an empty {@link Optional} if a match could not be made.
333    * @throws ServletException If a lazy child fails to materialize.
334    */
335   public Optional<RestChildMatch> findMatch(RestSession.Builder builder) throws ServletException {
336      var pi = builder.getPathInfoUndecoded();
337      if (nn(pi) && ! pi.equals("/")) {
338         // Check eager children first.
339         var snapshot = children;  // single volatile read
340         for (var rc : snapshot.values()) {
341            var pm = rc.getPathMatcher();  // Treated as nullable per RestContext's own usage (see RestContext.getPathMatcher() null-guards).
342            UrlPathMatch uppm = pm == null ? null : pm.match(builder.getUrlPath());
343            if (nn(uppm))
344               return opt(RestChildMatch.create(uppm, rc));
345         }
346         // Check lazy entries.
347         for (var e : lazyEntries.values()) {
348            UrlPathMatch uppm = e.pathMatcher.match(builder.getUrlPath());
349            if (nn(uppm)) {
350               var rc = e.materialize();
351               return opt(RestChildMatch.create(uppm, rc));
352            }
353         }
354      }
355      return opte();
356   }
357
358   /**
359    * Called during servlet initialization on all eager children to invoke all {@link RestPostInit} child-last methods.
360    *
361    * <p>
362    * Lazy children are intentionally excluded — they are initialized on first invocation, not at startup.
363    *
364    * @throws ServletException Error occurred.
365    */
366   public void postInit() throws ServletException {
367      for (var childContext : children.values())
368         childContext.postInit();
369   }
370
371   /**
372    * Called during servlet initialization on all eager children to invoke all {@link RestPostInit} child-first methods.
373    *
374    * <p>
375    * Lazy children are intentionally excluded — they are initialized on first invocation, not at startup.
376    *
377    * @throws ServletException Error occurred.
378    */
379   public void postInitChildFirst() throws ServletException {
380      for (var childContext : children.values())
381         childContext.postInitChildFirst();
382   }
383
384   /**
385    * Returns the lazy child entries registered on this object.
386    *
387    * <p>
388    * Each entry carries the path prefix and materialization state. Primarily used for testing.
389    *
390    * @return An unmodifiable map of path-to-lazy-entry. Never {@code null}.
391    */
392   public Map<String,LazyChildEntry> getLazyEntries() {
393      return lazyEntries;
394   }
395
396   //-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
397   // Dynamic add/remove API.
398   //
399   // Routing reads (findMatch / asMap) are lock-free against the volatile `children` snapshot.
400   // Mutations synchronize on writeLock and atomically swap in a new unmodifiable LinkedHashMap.
401   //-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
402
403   /**
404    * Dynamically registers a child REST resource by class, instantiating it via the parent's {@link BeanStore}.
405    *
406    * <p>
407    * The path under which the child is registered is determined by the {@link Rest#path() @Rest(path)} annotation
408    * on the resource class composed against the parent's full path.
409    *
410    * <p>
411    * The new child's {@link RestContext#postInit()} and {@link RestContext#postInitChildFirst()} lifecycle hooks
412    * are invoked before this method returns, mirroring the eager-init behavior of {@code @Rest(children = ...)}.
413    *
414    * @param resourceClass The {@code @Rest}-annotated resource class.
415    * @return The newly-built child {@link RestContext}.
416    * @throws ServletException If construction or lifecycle initialization of the child fails.
417    * @throws IllegalStateException If a child is already registered at the resolved path.
418    */
419   public RestContext addChild(Class<?> resourceClass) throws ServletException {
420      return addChildInternal(resourceClass, null, "", false);
421   }
422
423   /**
424    * Dynamically registers a pre-instantiated child REST resource.
425    *
426    * <p>
427    * The path under which the child is registered is determined by the {@link Rest#path() @Rest(path)} annotation
428    * on the resource's class composed against the parent's full path.
429    *
430    * @param resource The {@code @Rest}-annotated resource instance.
431    * @return The newly-built child {@link RestContext}.
432    * @throws ServletException If construction or lifecycle initialization of the child fails.
433    * @throws IllegalStateException If a child is already registered at the resolved path.
434    */
435   public RestContext addChild(Object resource) throws ServletException {
436      return addChildInternal(resource.getClass(), resource, "", false);
437   }
438
439   /**
440    * Dynamically registers a pre-instantiated child REST resource, optionally replacing any existing child at the
441    * same resolved path.
442    *
443    * @param resource The {@code @Rest}-annotated resource instance.
444    * @param replace If {@code true}, an existing child at the same path is destroyed and removed before the new
445    *    child is added. If {@code false}, an existing child causes an {@link IllegalStateException}.
446    * @return The newly-built child {@link RestContext}.
447    * @throws ServletException If construction or lifecycle initialization of the child fails.
448    */
449   public RestContext addChild(Object resource, boolean replace) throws ServletException {
450      return addChildInternal(resource.getClass(), resource, "", replace);
451   }
452
453   /**
454    * Dynamically registers a pre-instantiated child REST resource at an explicit path.
455    *
456    * <p>
457    * The supplied {@code path} overrides whatever {@link Rest#path() @Rest(path)} would normally provide. Useful
458    * for mounting the same servlet class at multiple paths, or for test fixtures that compose paths programmatically.
459    *
460    * @param path The path segment under the parent at which to mount this child. Leading slashes are trimmed.
461    * @param resource The {@code @Rest}-annotated resource instance.
462    * @return The newly-built child {@link RestContext}.
463    * @throws ServletException If construction or lifecycle initialization of the child fails.
464    * @throws IllegalStateException If a child is already registered at the resolved path.
465    */
466   public RestContext addChild(String path, Object resource) throws ServletException {
467      return addChildInternal(resource.getClass(), resource, path, false);
468   }
469
470   /**
471    * Dynamically registers a pre-instantiated child REST resource at an explicit path, optionally replacing any
472    * existing child at the same path.
473    *
474    * @param path The path segment under the parent at which to mount this child. Leading slashes are trimmed.
475    * @param resource The {@code @Rest}-annotated resource instance.
476    * @param replace If {@code true}, an existing child at the same path is destroyed and removed before the new
477    *    child is added. If {@code false}, an existing child causes an {@link IllegalStateException}.
478    * @return The newly-built child {@link RestContext}.
479    * @throws ServletException If construction or lifecycle initialization of the child fails.
480    */
481   public RestContext addChild(String path, Object resource, boolean replace) throws ServletException {
482      return addChildInternal(resource.getClass(), resource, path, replace);
483   }
484
485   /**
486    * Removes the child registered at the given composed path.
487    *
488    * <p>
489    * The removed child has {@link RestContext#destroy()} invoked (which runs {@code @RestDestroy} hooks and
490    * recursively destroys any grandchildren), followed by {@link Servlet#destroy()} if the underlying resource is
491    * a {@link Servlet}.
492    *
493    * @param path The composed path key (as returned by {@link RestContext#getPath()}) of the child to remove.
494    * @return The removed and destroyed {@link RestContext}, or {@code null} if no child was registered at the
495    *    given path.
496    */
497   public RestContext removeChild(String path) {
498      RestContext removed;
499      synchronized (writeLock) {
500         removed = children.get(path);
501         if (removed == null)
502            return null;
503         var next = new LinkedHashMap<>(children);
504         next.remove(path);
505         children = Collections.unmodifiableMap(next);
506      }
507      destroyChild(removed);
508      return removed;
509   }
510
511   /**
512    * Removes the first child whose resource class matches the given type.
513    *
514    * @param resourceClass The resource class to match. Matching is performed via {@link RestContext#getResourceClass()}.
515    * @return The removed and destroyed {@link RestContext}, or {@code null} if no matching child was found.
516    */
517   public RestContext removeChild(Class<?> resourceClass) {
518      String key = null;
519      RestContext removed = null;
520      synchronized (writeLock) {
521         for (var e : children.entrySet()) {
522            if (e.getValue().getResourceClass() == resourceClass) {
523               key = e.getKey();
524               removed = e.getValue();
525               break;
526            }
527         }
528         if (removed == null)
529            return null;
530         var next = new LinkedHashMap<>(children);
531         next.remove(key);
532         children = Collections.unmodifiableMap(next);
533      }
534      destroyChild(removed);
535      return removed;
536   }
537
538   //-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
539   // Internal helpers.
540   //-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
541
542   /**
543    * Shared child-context construction recipe — used both by the eager init memoizer in {@link RestContext} and by
544    * the runtime {@link #addChild(Class) addChild} family of methods.
545    *
546    * <p>
547    * Resolves a {@link Supplier} for the resource (prefers an existing bean of the same class in the bean store,
548    * otherwise instantiates via {@link BeanInstantiator}), constructs the child {@link RestContext} using the
549    * supplied {@code parent} / {@code servletConfig} / {@code pathOverride}, and invokes any
550    * {@code setContext(RestContext)} method on the resource via reflection (so that {@code RestServlet}-style
551    * resources receive their context handle).
552    *
553    * @param parent The parent {@link RestContext}. Must not be {@code null}.
554    * @param beanStore The bean store to resolve instances and dependencies from. Must not be {@code null}.
555    * @param servletConfig The {@link ServletConfig} to pass through to the child. May be {@code null}.
556    * @param resourceClass The resource class. Must not be {@code null}.
557    * @param resourceInstance A pre-supplied instance of the resource. If {@code null}, the instance is resolved
558    *    from the bean store or freshly instantiated via {@link BeanInstantiator}.
559    * @param pathOverride An explicit path segment (relative to the parent). Use {@code ""} to read the path from
560    *    {@link Rest#path() @Rest(path)} on the resource class.
561    * @return The newly-built child {@link RestContext}, with its {@code setContext} method (if any) invoked.
562    * @throws Exception If construction or reflective wiring fails.
563    */
564   static RestContext buildChildContext(
565         RestContext parent,
566         BeanStore beanStore,
567         ServletConfig servletConfig,
568         Class<?> resourceClass,
569         Object resourceInstance,
570         String pathOverride) throws Exception {
571      Supplier<?> so;
572      if (resourceInstance != null) {
573         final Object r = resourceInstance;
574         so = () -> r;
575      } else if (beanStore.getBean(resourceClass).isPresent()) {
576         so = () -> beanStore.getBean(resourceClass).get();
577      } else {
578         Object o = BeanInstantiator.of(resourceClass, beanStore).run();
579         so = () -> o;
580      }
581      var cc = new RestContext(new RestContext.Args(resourceClass, parent, servletConfig, so, pathOverride, null, null, null, RestContext.ContextKind.CHILD));
582      var mi = ClassInfo.of(so.get())
583         .getMethod(x -> x.hasName("setContext") && x.hasParameterTypes(RestContext.class))
584         .orElse(null);
585      if (nn(mi))
586         mi.accessible().invoke(so.get(), cc);
587      return cc;
588   }
589
590   private RestContext addChildInternal(Class<?> resourceClass, Object resourceInstance, String pathOverride, boolean replace) throws ServletException {
591      if (parent == null || beanStore == null)
592         throw illegalState("Cannot add a child to a RestChildren that was not initialized with a parent RestContext.");
593      RestContext cc;
594      try {
595         cc = buildChildContext(parent, beanStore, servletConfig, resourceClass, resourceInstance, pathOverride);
596      } catch (Exception e) {
597         throw new ServletException("Failed to build child REST context for " + resourceClass.getName(), unwrapThrowable(e));
598      }
599      var key = cc.getPath();
600      RestContext replaced = null;
601      synchronized (writeLock) {
602         if (children.containsKey(key)) {
603            if (! replace) {
604               destroyQuietly(cc);
605               throw illegalState("Child resource already registered at path ''{0}''.", key);
606            }
607            replaced = children.get(key);
608            var withoutExisting = new LinkedHashMap<>(children);
609            withoutExisting.remove(key);
610            // We don't publish the intermediate "without existing" snapshot — single atomic swap below.
611            withoutExisting.put(key, cc);
612            children = Collections.unmodifiableMap(withoutExisting);
613         } else {
614            var next = new LinkedHashMap<>(children);
615            next.put(key, cc);
616            children = Collections.unmodifiableMap(next);
617         }
618      }
619      if (replaced != null)
620         destroyChild(replaced);
621      cc.postInit();
622      cc.postInitChildFirst();
623      return cc;
624   }
625
626   private static void destroyChild(RestContext child) {
627      // Servlet-backed resources delegate Servlet.destroy() through to RestContext.destroy() (see RestServlet.destroy),
628      // so we must avoid double-destruction in that case — call Servlet.destroy() and let it transitively call
629      // RestContext.destroy() exactly once.
630      if (child.getResource() instanceof Servlet s)
631         s.destroy();
632      else
633         child.destroy();
634   }
635
636   private static void destroyQuietly(RestContext child) {
637      try {
638         destroyChild(child);
639      } catch (Exception ignored) {
640         // best-effort cleanup of a child we're about to throw away
641      }
642   }
643
644   private static Throwable unwrapThrowable(Throwable t) {
645      if (t instanceof InvocationTargetException t2)
646         return t2.getTargetException();
647      return t;
648   }
649}
